title: 🔴 1D Dynamic Programming — Dry Run & Quiz
oneSentence: Trace Climbing Stairs, Coin Change, and House Robber.

climbStairs.title: Climbing Stairs
climbStairs.description: Find the number of ways to climb to the top.
climbStairs.story: Each step builds upon the previous two.

steps.desc: Loop starts. Compute third = first + second.

quiz.q: What is the relation between Climbing Stairs and Fibonacci sequence?
options: They are identical recurrence relations: f(n) = f(n-1) + f(n-2).
quiz.explain: Both problems rely on summing the results of the two immediately preceding states.

coinChange.title: Coin Change
coinChange.description: Find minimum coins to make up an amount.
coinChange.story: Build the smallest wallet for each amount.
code: public int coinChange(int[] coins, int amount) {

steps.desc: Create dp array of size amount + 1.
Fill dp array with amount + 1 (acts as infinity).
Base case: dp[0] = 0 coins needed for amount 0.

quiz.q: Why fill the DP array with `amount + 1` instead of `Integer.MAX_VALUE`?
options: To prevent integer overflow when adding 1 to the DP state.
Because amount + 1 is always the exact answer.
quiz.explain: If `dp[i - coin]` was `Integer.MAX_VALUE`, adding 1 would cause integer overflow and result in a negative number.

houseRobber.title: House Robber
houseRobber.description: Maximize loot without robbing adjacent houses.
houseRobber.story: To rob or not to rob?

steps.desc: Initialize two variables to track max loot.
Decide: rob current house (n + rob1) or skip it (rob2).

quiz.q: What does rob1 represent in the loop?
options: The maximum loot excluding the immediate previous house.
The maximum loot including the current house.
The index of the last robbed house.
quiz.explain: rob1 represents the max loot excluding the immediate previous house, allowing us to safely add the current house's value.
